name: Hawaii Plantation Village
tagline: Walk through restored plantation-era homes and learn how Hawaii's multicultural identity was built.
hours: Mon–Sat 10am–2pm. Closed Sundays. Guided tours at 10am, 11am, 12pm, 1pm
bestTime: First tour at 10am is least crowded and cooler
talkStory: Founded in 1992 on the grounds of the former Oahu Sugar Company, this open-air museum preserves over 30 restored buildings representing eight ethnic groups who labored on Hawaii's sugar plantations from the 1850s to the 1940s. The volunteer docents are often descendants of plantation workers sharing personal family stories. Don't miss the restored Shinto shrine and Portuguese bread oven, both original to the era.
